chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – Satisfaction with the Judgement (of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/15)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/15)
text: لَمْ يَكُنْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَقُولُ لِشَيْ‏ءٍ قَدْ مَضَى لَوْ كَانَ غَيْرُهُ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Ibn Sinan, from Al Husayn Bin Al Mukhtar, from Abdullah in Abu Yafour,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘It did not happen that Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> was saying for something which had transpired: ‘If only something else would have happened’’.
en.sarwar: 13. It is narrated from him from his father from ibn Sinan from al-Husayn ibn al- Muhtar from ‘Abd Allah ibn abu Ya’fur from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah (a.s.) never said about something that had taken place, ‘Would that something else had happened.’”
